data.title: SoFi Technologies Q4 Earnings: Beat estimates with EPS of $0.13, revenue up $273.72 million. More details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Fintech firm SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ: SOFI) released its Q4 earnings on Friday, January 30, 2026, at 07:00 AM. data.title: Canadian National Railway Q4 earnings beat estimates by 4%, reporting EPS of $1.49. Revenue up by $86 million.

The results exceeded expectations, with an earnings per share (EPS) of $1.49 compared to the estimated $1.43, marking a 4.0% beat. Revenue also saw a significant increase, rising by $86.00 million from the previous year. More details about the company’s performance can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Air Products & Chemicals Inc reported Q1 2026 revenue of $3.103 billion, up 6% YoY, with an adjusted EPS of $3.16, beating estimates. Operating income rose 14% to $735 million. CEO cites pricing power and cost cuts for success.
data.description: Air Products & Chemicals Inc reported strong first-quarter fiscal 2026 results, with revenue reaching $3.103 billion, up 6% from the previous year and surpassing analyst estimates of $3.051 billion. Adjusted EPS stood at $3.16, a 10% increase year over year, beating the estimated $3.04. GAAP operating income rose to $735 million, up 14%, and GAAP EPS increased to $3.04 from $2.77. Adjusted operating income also saw a 12% rise to $757 million. The GAAP operating margin increased to 23.7%, while the adjusted operating margin rose to 24.4%. CEO Eduardo Menezes highlighted the company's performance, citing factors such as energy cost pass-through, favorable currency, and pricing contributing to sales growth despite flat volumes.

data.title: Denmark's BNPL market projected to grow 16.8% by 2026, reaching $8.12 billion. Robust growth seen during 2022-2025.
data.description: A new report on Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) business and investment opportunities in Denmark has been released, containing over 90 key performance indicators (KPIs) on market size, end-use sectors, market share, product analysis, business model, and demographics. The BNPL payment market in Denmark is projected to increase by 16.8% annually, reaching a total of US$8.12 billion by 2026. The country's buy now pay later market saw significant growth between 2022 and 2025.

data.title: Soundtrace passes review against OSHA requirements; aligned with 29 CFR 1910.95. Technology firm enhancing hearing conservation programs.
data.description: In Cincinnati on January 30, 2026, Soundtrace, a company specializing in modernizing hearing conservation and occupational noise programs, revealed the positive outcome of an evaluation conducted by a previous senior leader from OSHA. The assessment was based on compliance with 29 CFR 1910.95. This evaluation signifies Soundtrace's commitment to improving workplace safety and protecting employees from noise-related hazards.

data.title: Sundream, a platform of Plumbing and HVAC service businesses, acquires St. Augustine-based APS. (Character count: 91)
data.description: Viking Mergers & Acquisitions recently facilitated a significant deal in St. Augustine, Florida. Sundream, a platform consisting of various Plumbing and HVAC service entities, successfully acquired Atlantic Plumbing Services (APS). The acquisition sees Sundream expanding its reach in plumbing services within the region. APS, a reputable St. Augustine-based plumbing company, specialized in serving residential, commercial, and new construction properties. The deal, closed on January 30, 2026, marks a strategic move by Sundream to enhance its market presence.

data.title: C3.ai stock up nearly 16% in pre-market trading as talks of a merger with Automation Anywhere emerge. Deal could result in Automation Anywhere acquiring C3.ai and going public. C3.ai offers enterprise AI software; Automation Anywhere valued at $6.8 billion in 2019. C3.ai founder Thomas Siebel steps down as CEO amid merger talks.
data.description: C3.ai Inc. saw its stock (NYSE: AI) experience a significant surge of 15.87% in the pre-market trading session on Wednesday. Reports have emerged indicating that discussions are underway for a potential merger between C3.ai, an enterprise AI software provider, and Automation Anywhere, a software company. If this merger indeed materializes, it would entail Automation Anywhere acquiring C3.ai and subsequently launching as a publicly traded entity. C3.ai specializes in supplying enterprise AI software utilized by various clients, including the likes of the U.S. Air Force, operating across sectors such as energy and manufacturing. On the other hand, Automation Anywhere is focused on developing task-automation software and was previously valued at $6.8 billion by private investors in 2019. Following the departure of C3.ai's founder, Thomas Siebel, from the CEO position, the company is yet to formally comment on this potential deal. This development marks a notable event in the industry landscape and the full details can be found on platforms like Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stocks surge with Nasdaq eyeing record high; Dow Jones Futures up. Tesla, Meta, Microsoft in spotlight. Fed's Powell to speak.
data.description: Numerous stocks showed upward momentum with the Nasdaq leading the way and the S&P 500 reaching a peak. However, attention is now turning to key players such as Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Dow Jones Futures are indicating a positive outlook with the Nasdaq potentially setting a new all-time high. These developments come amid a dynamic market environment where investor focus is on influential companies and potential market-moving events.

data.title: CN (CNR) increases dividend by 3% and launches share repurchase program under new normal course issuer bid.
data.description: In Montreal on January 30, 2026, CN (CNR on TSX, CNI on NYSE) revealed a 3% surge in the 2026 dividend for its common shares, sanctioned by the Board of Directors. Additionally, the Board approved the repurchase of company shares through a freshly implemented normal course issuer bid.
